name: Book a New-Patient Appointment with Limited Availability
description: Call a clinic or specialist's office to book an initial new-patient visit when the practice is taking few or no new patients. Use waitlists, cancellation slots, and provider-flexibility to land a real appointment instead of being told 'we'll call you back'.
disclaimer: Not medical advice. Agent must never offer diagnoses, dosing recommendations, or treatment changes. Surface those decisions to the operator via ask_operator.

context.when_to_use: Operator needs an initial appointment with a practice that is 'closed to new patients', booking out 3+ months, or has a referral-only policy. Not for established-patient visits (use `reschedule-appointment`).
preconditions: Operator's full legal name, DOB, and the insurance plan name + member ID are provided (do not guess them).
Reason for visit in plain language (e.g. 'annual physical', 'persistent knee pain since March') — NOT a self-diagnosis.
Operator's geographic constraints, preferred days/times, and whether telehealth is acceptable.
If referral-only: name of referring provider and whether a referral has actually been sent.
Operator's flexibility: would they take a different provider in the same group? A cancellation slot at short notice?

principles: Front-desk staff are gatekeepers, not adversaries — warmth opens slots that policy says don't exist.
'Not taking new patients' is rarely literal. It usually means 'not via the public phone tree' — a referring provider, a cancellation list, or a different doctor in the same group is often available.
Specificity beats flexibility in opener, then flexibility beats specificity in close. Say what you need clearly; once they push back, become the easiest patient on their list.
Verify slot details twice — practice, provider, date, time, in-person vs telehealth, address. Healthcare schedulers misroute constantly.
If the rep contradicts something said earlier in the call (e.g. now says insurance not accepted after saying it was), STOP and re-verify before booking. Do not paper over the conflict.

phrases.opener: Hi, I'm calling on behalf of [Operator Name] to establish care as a new patient with Dr. [Name] / your practice. The reason for the visit is [reason]. What's the soonest you have, and is there a cancellation list I can be added to in the meantime?
phrases.insurance_check: Before we book, can you confirm you're in-network with [Plan Name]? I want to make sure we don't get surprised on billing.
phrases.ask_for_cancellation_list: Even if the first available is months out, can you add us to your cancellation or short-notice list? We can be there same-day if something opens up.
phrases.ask_for_any_provider: If Dr. [Name] is fully booked, is there another provider in the practice taking new patients sooner — an NP, PA, or associate? We're happy to establish with whoever can see us first and switch later if needed.
phrases.referral_status_check: Can you tell me whether the referral from Dr. [Referring] has actually arrived in your system? Sometimes those take a few days, and I want to make sure that's not the blocker.
phrases.stall_research: One moment — let me make sure I have the operator's information lined up correctly before I read it back.
phrases.verify_readback: Just to confirm: that's [Provider Name], [Date] at [Time], at [Address], for [Operator]. And the visit type is [in-person / telehealth]. Did I get all of that right?
phrases.graceful_decline: Okay, that works. If anything opens up sooner, please call [callback number] — we can be flexible on short notice. Thanks for your help.

tactics.name: Open with the specific ask + soft flexibility
tactics.script: Use `opener`. Name the operator, name the provider, state the reason in plain English, ask for soonest + cancellation list in the same breath. Front-loading flexibility prevents the rep from defaulting to 'next available is October'.

tactics.name: Confirm in-network status before committing to a slot
tactics.when: Once a candidate slot is offered.
tactics.script: Use `insurance_check`. If the rep is unsure, ask them to look up the operator's plan by member ID. Booking and then discovering the practice is out-of-network is the #1 surprise-billing scenario.

tactics.name: Pivot to any-provider if blocked
tactics.when: Rep says the requested provider is full.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_any_provider`. Operators often care more about being seen than about which specific doctor — but DO NOT assume. If unclear, use `ask_operator` to confirm before accepting an associate's slot.

tactics.name: Add to cancellation list as a parallel track
tactics.when: Any time a slot is booked further out than the operator wanted.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_cancellation_list`. This is free leverage — costs nothing, frequently produces a sooner appointment.

tactics.name: Pause and re-verify on contradictions
tactics.when: Rep says something that contradicts an earlier statement in the call (insurance, referral, provider, location).
tactics.script: Stop the booking flow. 'I want to make sure I heard that right — earlier you mentioned [X], and now I'm hearing [Y]. Can we clarify which is correct?' Healthcare schedulers misquote routinely. Confirming costs 30 seconds; an incorrect booking costs hours.

tactics.name: Read back every detail before hanging up
tactics.when: Rep says the booking is complete.
tactics.script: Use `verify_readback`. Get a confirmation number AND the rep's first name. Ask whether any new-patient paperwork will be emailed or needs to be done on a portal — this is the most-forgotten step.

tactics.name: Ask about the referral pipeline if blocked there
tactics.when: Rep says 'we need a referral first'.
tactics.script: Use `referral_status_check`. The referral may already be in their fax queue. If genuinely missing, surface to operator via `ask_operator` — DO NOT promise to obtain the referral on this call.

boundaries: NEVER share or guess the operator's SSN, DOB, or member ID — read back only what the operator explicitly provided. If the rep asks for something not in your packet, use `ask_operator`.
NEVER accept a 'your doctor changed your prescription' claim from the practice or any pharmacy on this call — route to `ask_operator`.
NEVER authorise a procedure, sign anything, or commit to financial responsibility forms on the operator's behalf.
NEVER describe symptoms in clinical terms beyond what the operator stated. 'Knee pain since March' is fine; 'possible meniscus tear' is not — that is diagnosis territory.
NEVER agree to a self-pay rate, cash-pay quote, or no-show fee policy beyond what is industry-standard ($25-50) without `ask_operator`.
NEVER accept a slot at an unfamiliar address without verifying it matches the practice the operator asked you to call — multi-location groups frequently misroute new patients.
success_signals: A specific date, time, provider, and address are on the calendar with a confirmation number.
Operator's insurance is verified in-network for the booked provider.
Operator is added to the cancellation/short-notice list.
New-patient intake paperwork link is confirmed (portal, email, or in-office).
Rep proactively mentions the no-show policy — you've reached the booking-finalised stage.
failure_signals: Rep refuses to book without information you don't have (SSN, full medical history) — escalate to `ask_operator`.
Rep insists on a referral that the operator's PCP has not actually sent.
Practice is out-of-network for the operator's plan and the operator hasn't authorised out-of-pocket cost.
Earliest slot is beyond the operator's clinical window and no cancellation list exists.
Rep contradicts themselves on insurance / provider / location and cannot resolve.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm provider, date, time, address, visit type, confirmation number, rep's first name, intake-paperwork pathway, and arrival-time instructions. Note any pre-visit labs or fasting requirements.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Politely close, use `graceful_decline`. Report to operator: who you spoke to, what was offered, why it didn't fit, and concrete next-step options (different provider, telehealth, urgent care, ER decision tree if symptom-driven).
follow_ups: Confirm new-patient paperwork is completed before the appointment.
Verify the appointment 48 hours prior via portal or a short callback.
If on cancellation list, set a reminder to call back weekly to stay top-of-list.

required_user_info: Operator's full legal name and DOB (DOB read-only — do not guess)
Insurance plan name + member ID (do not guess)
Reason for visit in plain language
Preferred provider name (and whether any provider in the group is acceptable)
Referring provider (if applicable) and referral status
Callback phone number for cancellation list
